name: Decap CMS
description: Git-based open-source CMS for static site generators with visual editing

description.value: Git-based open-source CMS for static site generators with visual editing

initial-release.comment: Originally created as Netlify CMS in 2015, rebranded to Decap CMS in February 2023

cms-type.comment: Git-based headless CMS that stores content in Git repositories

deployment-model.comment: Single-page React app deployed to your site's /admin folder; no managed SaaS offering

database-support.comment: No database; content stored in Git repositories as flat files (Markdown, JSON, YAML, TOML)

api-type.comment: Uses GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket REST APIs as backend; no dedicated content API

multi-tenancy.comment: Single site per installation; each site has its own config.yml

static-site-generation.comment: Designed specifically for static site generators (JAMstack)

edge-deployment.comment: Static output deploys to CDN/edge; admin UI is a static SPA

content-types.comment: Define custom collections with arbitrary content structures via config.yml

custom-fields.comment: 16+ built-in widgets plus custom widget support

field-types.comment: String, Text, Markdown, Number, Boolean, DateTime, Image, File, List (repeater), Object (component), Relation (reference), Select, Code, Color, Map, Hidden

content-relationships.comment: Relation widget enables search-enabled dropdown referencing items from other collections

nested-components.comment: Object and List widgets support nested widgets for component-like structures

localization.comment: Built-in i18n support with multiple storage structures (multiple folders, multiple files, single file)

content-validation.comment: Fields support required, pattern (regex), and custom validation via isValid method

embedded-interactives.comment: registerEditorComponent allows block-level components in Markdown editor

content-versioning.comment: Inherent through Git; all content changes are Git commits with full history

draft-publish.comment: Editorial workflow creates draft branches and pull requests for review

scheduled-publishing.comment: No native scheduled publishing; would require external automation

approval-workflows.comment: Editorial workflow supports draft, review, and approve/publish stages via PR-based workflow

content-locking.comment: No content locking; relies on Git conflict resolution

audit-logging.comment: Inherent through Git commit history; all changes tracked with author and timestamp

media-library.comment: Built-in media library with drag-and-drop uploads

image-transformations.comment: No built-in transformations; requires external services like Cloudinary

external-media-storage.comment: Supports Cloudinary, Netlify Large Media, and Uploadcare integrations

video-support.comment: File widget supports any file type including video

svg-support.comment: Image widget explicitly supports svg format

folder-organization.comment: Media folder configurable; file organization follows Git repository structure

role-based-access.comment: No built-in roles; authentication handled by Git provider or Netlify Identity

granular-permissions.comment: No field-level or collection-level permissions in the CMS itself

sso-saml.comment: Relies on Git provider OAuth; no native SSO/SAML

team-collaboration.comment: No comments or assignments in CMS; collaboration happens via Git PRs

typescript-sdk.comment: CMS API is JavaScript-based; limited TypeScript support

content-type-sdk.comment: Content types defined in YAML config.yml, not programmatically

cli-tools.comment: No CLI; installed by adding files to your site or via npm package

local-development.comment: local_backend setting enables local Git operations during development

webhooks.comment: No native webhook support; event listeners can trigger custom code but webhooks are a requested feature

plugin-system.comment: Extensible via registerWidget, registerEditorComponent, and custom backends

admin-ui-customization.comment: Customizable logo, preview templates, custom widgets, and editor components

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate docs covering main features; some gaps in advanced topics; community-maintained since rebrand

frontend-frameworks.comment: Official guides for Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby; works with any SSG that outputs to Git

search-integration.comment: Built-in search for filtering content in the admin UI; can disable if rate limits are a concern

preview-mode.comment: Real-time preview in editor; deploy preview links for published sites

ecommerce-integration.comment: No built-in e-commerce integrations; content-only CMS

cdn-included.comment: Self-hosted; CDN depends on your hosting provider

response-caching.comment: No API response caching; uses Git provider APIs directly

rate-limits.comment: Subject to GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket API rate limits

free-tier.comment: Fully free and open source under MIT license

open-source.comment: MIT licensed, maintained by community since Netlify CMS rebrand

pricing-model.comment: Completely free; optional paid professional services available

commercial-support.comment: Professional services available for priority support, onboarding, and custom features

enterprise-features.comment: No enterprise-specific features (SSO, SLA, dedicated support tiers)
